results.title: Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling: A 975B-Parameter Open-Weights Multimodal MoE With 41B Active Parameters And Controllable Thinking Effort - MarkTechPost
results.content:
# Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling: A 975B-Parameter Open-Weights Multimodal MoE With 41B Active Parameters And Controllable Thinking Effort

By

Asif Razzaq

-

Thinking Machines Lab just released Inkling, their first model trained from scratch, weights are open, fine-tunable on Tinker. The lab pitches it as a base for customization.

## What is Inkling?

Inkling is a Mixture-of-Experts transformer with 975B total parameters and 41B active. It supports a context window of up to 1M tokens. Pretraining covered 45 trillion tokens of text, images, audio, and video. Inputs accept text, images, and audio; output is UTF-8 text only. [...] # pip install -U transformers (5.14.0 or later) from transformers import AutoModelForMultimodalLM, AutoProcessor model_id = "thinkingmachines/Inkling" # BF16, Hopper or later # model_id = "thinkingmachines/Inkling-NVFP4" # NVFP4, Blackwell processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(model_id) model = AutoModelForMultimodalLM.from_pretrained( model_id, dtype="auto", device_map="auto", ) messages = [ {"role": "user", "content": [ {"type": "audio", "audio": "support_call.wav"}, # 16kHz WAV {"type": "text", "text": "Transcribe, then list every billing complaint."}, ]}, ] inputs = processor.apply_chat_template( messages, add_generation_prompt=True, tokenize=True, return_dict=True, return_tensors="pt", # none | minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh | max reasoning_effort="medium", [...] OpenAI-compatible serving takes one command:

Copy CodeCopiedUse a different Browser

```
vllm serve thinkingmachines/Inkling --tensor-parallel-size 8 --served-model-name inkling
```

For fine-tuning, Inkling is live on Tinker with 64K and 256K context options. The research team also released `tml-renderers` for post-training with tool calls and multimodal inputs. Hosted APIs exist via TogetherAI, Fireworks, Modal, Databricks, and Baseten.

Given those constraints, three deployment patterns follow.

## Where Inkling Fits: Use Cases

results.content:
🎯 In this video, we explore:

✅ Inkling’s 975B-parameter MoE architecture
⚡ 41B active parameters for efficient inference
📚 Massive 1M-token context window
💻 Coding and advanced reasoning capabilities
🤖 Agentic coding and tool usage
🖼️ Image and audio understanding
🎚️ Controllable thinking effort
🔓 Open weights for developers and researchers
🛠️ Fine-tuning support through Tinker
⚔️ Inkling compared with other frontier AI models
🔍 Inkling-Small and the future of the model family

Inkling is designed for developers building AI agents, coding assistants, chatbots, retrieval-augmented generation systems, multimodal applications, and custom fine-tuned AI models. [...] # 🚀 Inkling: 975B Open-Weight Multimodal AI Model by Thinking Machines Lab | 1M Context
## GATE And Tech 
3880 subscribers
1 likes

### Description
2 views
Posted: 20 Jul 2026
🚀 Thinking Machines Lab has officially introduced Inkling, its first open-weight, general-purpose multimodal AI model.

🧠 Inkling is a massive Mixture-of-Experts model featuring 975 billion total parameters, with 41 billion parameters active during inference. It supports a context window of up to 1 million tokens and was pretrained on 45 trillion tokens of text, images, audio, and video.

Inkling can process:

📝 Text
🖼️ Images
🎙️ Audio
💬 Text-based outputs

🎯 In this video, we explore: [...] ▶️ Watch the complete video to learn how Inkling works, explore its major capabilities, and discover whether it can compete with today’s leading open and closed AI models.

## Trending now

results.title: Instagram
results.content:
Mira Murati’s Thinking Machines Lab just released Inkling — a 975B open weights AI model anyone can download and fine-tune.  
It’s the first model the ex-OpenAI CTO’s lab has trained from scratch, and the first serious US answer to Kimi, GLM, DeepSeek and Qwen on the open source AI leaderboard.  
  
What Inkling actually is:  
→ Mixture-of-Experts LLM — 975B total parameters, 41B active  
→ 1M token context window  
→ Natively multimodal — reasons over text, images and audio  
→ Pretrained on 45 trillion tokens  
→ Full open weights on Hugging Face  
  
The honest part: Thinking Machines says outright it’s not the strongest model available today, closed or open. So why does it matter?
